content: We can frame naturally occurring biological agents (such as invasive bacteria or spreading viral populations) as swarm autonomous agents that rely on a biological analog of the 2025 configurable ICM curiosity mechanism to drive their exploratory spread. Biological agents gain an intrinsic 'reward' for encountering uncolonized host tissues or new host species, which drives them to explore high-uncertainty environments that eventually lead to new niche colonization and adaptive evolution.
